title: 6 ATM Deposits â Issuer Procedures
body_text:
6	ATM Deposits â Issuer Procedures
6.1	Overview of ATM Deposit Procedures for the Issuer
The financial institution that issues the debit card is the issuer. Cardholders can deposit cheques at an ATM owned by
any participant of THE EXCHANGE
Â®
, or
any other Canadian financial institution that participates in the ACCULINK
Â®
network.
See Section 5.3, Route and Transit and AFT Adjustment Account Listings, for procedures on obtaining the appropriate route and transit listings.
6.2	Receiving Notification of Exception Deposits
The acquirer of a deposit must notify the issuer immediately by phone or fax in the situations specified below. This notification allows you to hold funds on the deposit if this is your financial institutionâs policy. The acquirer must send notification no later than the business day following the settlement day of the deposit.
If you receive a phone call asking for confirmation, give the caller your name. If you receive a fax asking for confirmation, immediately send back an acknowledging reply, giving your name and phone number. Keep a record of all notifications.
THE EXCHANGE Exception Deposits
The Canadian Exchange Network Operating Rules stipulate that the acquirer must notify the cardholderâs financial institution as follows:
If the deposit value is $5,000 or greater, fax
â
a copy of the front and back of the singular cheque that is valued at $5,000 or greater,
â
a confirmation of a cash deposit greater than $5,000, if applicable, and
â
a duly completed EXCHANGE Network Advice Form.
If the deposit value is $10,000 or greater, fax
â
a copy of the front and back of all cheques that make up all or part of the deposit,
â
a confirmation of a cash deposit greater than $5,000, if applicable, and
â
a duly completed EXCHANGE Network Advice Form.
If itâs evident that a cheque has been materially altered, regardless of whether the alteration is initialed, fax
â
a copy of the front and back of the cheque, and
â
a duly completed EXCHANGE Network Advice Form.
If a debit or credit adjustment to a deposit is required because the difference is $20.01 or greater, fax a duly completed EXCHANGE Network Advice Form.
If an empty deposit envelope is received, fax a duly completed EXCHANGE Network Advice Form.
ACCULINK Exception Deposits
ACCULINK ATM Operating Policies and Procedures stipulate that the acquirer must notify the issuer if:
A deposit contains an error of $20.01 or greater.
An empty deposit envelope is received.
The deposit value is $10,000 or greater.
Note:	A copy of the front and back of all cheques that make up all or part of the deposit must be provided.
A cheque isnât encoded with the route and transit numbers.
Liability
If you receive a phone call or fax, you must acknowledge receipt either by exchanging names (if by phone) or by replying to the fax.
If the acquirer fails to give you notification, the acquirer may be responsible for any losses. However, if you fail to acknowledge the notification, the acquirer may not be responsible.
6.3	Chargebacks (Issuer)
The Canadian Exchange Network Operating Rules and the ACCULINK ATM Operating Policies and Procedures stipulate that if an acquirer receives a chargeback on a deposit, the acquirer must notify you by phone or confirmed facsimile, either
before the close of business on the day they received notice of the chargeback, or
the next business day that you are open.
6.4	Bill Payments (Issuer)
If one of your customers makes a bill payment at another financial institutionâs ATM and the payment is correctly keyed as a âpayment,â the acquirer will either remit the payment directly to the vendor or forward the payment and stub to you for processing.
If a bill payment is incorrectly keyed as a âdeposit,â the acquirer will send it back to you as an adjustment. See Chapter 11, Receiving ATM Traces and Adjustments.
END
